business.headline: CNG prices hiked by Rs 2 per kg in Mumbai, Thane, Navi MumbaiThis is the second hike in less than a month, following an earlier revision on May 14. The cumulative Rs 4 rise is expected to impact daily commuters, taxi operators and commercial vehicle users who rely heavily on CNG.

Illness, insurance and the cost of timingWhat happens when a chronic illness arrives before health insurance? One Multiple Sclerosis patient's journey highlights the challenges of getting coverage for pre-existing diseases in India.

Will Kwality Wall's big dairy push help it compete better in India?The company is making changes across products, pricing, factories and supply systems. Around half of Kwality Wall's products are expected to be dairy-based this year, while most of the shift will be completed next year.

Could Delhi HC's Hindware verdict reshape Google Ads and digital marketing in India?The Delhi High Court has ruled that Google cannot allow rival companies to bid on the trademarked keyword "HINDWARE" through its advertising platform.

Sensex, Nifty crash over inflation concern on poor rain forecastThe S&P BSE Sensex plunged 1,092.06 points, or 1.44%, to close at 74,775.74, while the NSE Nifty50 dropped 359.40 points, or 1.50%, to settle at 23,547.75.

Absolut Vodka maker battles Delhi ban as Rs 3,000 crore tax dispute deepensThe Delhi High Court has rejected Pernod Ricard's plea to resume liquor sales in the capital. The order adds to a separate customs dispute that has put nearly Rs 3,000 crore at stake.

How Gen Z and women buyers are turning India into the 2nd-largest diamond marketWhat if diamonds were no longer saved for "special occasions"? Across India, Gen Z shoppers and self-buying women are increasingly choosing diamonds as everyday luxury, a shift that is helping the country emerge as the world's second-largest diamond market.

Are India's stock investors overtaxed? Vijay Kedia thinks so, offers 3 solutionsVeteran investor Vijay Kedia has urged the Finance Ministry that the country's tax policy should reward long-term wealth creation instead of burdening those who provide patient capital to businesses.

From rising prices to rupee pressure: 5 key takeaways from RBI's annual reportIndia's economy remains strong, but challenges are growing. Slower growth, rising inflation risks and geopolitical tensions may test the economy in the coming months.

A war away, but not far away: RBI's warning for India's economyFor all the talk around India's strong economic growth, resilient consumers and healthy banks, the RBI's latest assessment contains a clear message: the biggest risks to the country's growth story are increasingly coming from outside its borders.

Under-construction or ready-to-move home: Which saves me more money in the long run?Buying a home is exciting, but one question often leaves buyers confused: should you go for a cheaper under-construction property or pay more for a ready-to-move home? Let's have a look.

Inspection Raj in the service of employers evading complianceIn the ninth part of this series on labour laws and Inspector Raj, this article examines how reduced scrutiny in the name of "ease of doing business" may create space for wilful labour law violations.

Why Delta Corp share price crashed 16% todayDelta Corp shares slid 16% after the Supreme Court upheld the government's retrospective 28 per cent GST levy on online gaming companies. The ruling has intensified concerns over large tax demands and compliance costs across the sector.

8th Pay Commission: Pensioners seek option to choose between OPS, NPS, UPSEmployee and pensioner groups have urged the 8th Pay Commission to let government staff choose between OPS, NPS and UPS. The demand has put retirement security and clarity over post-retirement income at the centre of the wider pay panel discussions.

Wipro jumps 5%, lifts IT stocks as AI deal sparks sector rallyWipro shares: Wipro stock gained as much as 4.76% during the day to Rs 211.20, while its American Depositary Receipts (ADRs) listed in the US had jumped more than 18% overnight.

IT stocks lead gains as Sensex, Nifty open higher on easing oil pricesThe S&P BSE Sensex rose 98.09 points, or 0.13%, to 75,965.89 in early trade, while the NSE Nifty50 gained 16.20 points, or 0.07%, to 23,923.35, as of 9:25 am.

SpaceX IPO: How Elon Musk's company is targeting a $1.8 trillion valuationSpaceX is discussing a valuation of about $1.8 trillion and a potential $75 billion raise ahead of its IPO. The filing shows the company is asking investors to see it as an AI infrastructure and space technology business.

Why India may be taking risky bet with $500 billion US dealMarco Rubio said in New Delhi that India had committed to buying $500 billion of American goods over five years. The remark has intensified scrutiny of whether the proposed arrangement offers India enough return.

The hidden salary leak Gen Z didn't notice until bank balances started shrinkingYoung salaried Indians are discovering that small app subscriptions are quietly eating into their monthly income. The online debate has sharpened focus on how auto-debits and convenience spending can distort budgeting.

Is E20 petrol quietly increasing your monthly fuel bill? 5 in 10 owners say yesA LocalCircles survey says many owners of older petrol vehicles have seen lower mileage after the E20 rollout. The findings sharpen consumer concerns over fuel costs, repairs and the shift towards higher ethanol blends.

entertainment.headline: Naga Chaitanya moves court against viral posts claiming he cheated Samantha The Delhi High Court issued notice on Naga Chaitanya's plea over alleged defamatory content about his past relationship with Samantha Ruth Prabhu. His counsel said the material amounted to trolling. The case also sought protection of his personality rights.

Ghooskhor Pandat row: Manoj Bajpayee says trolls abused him, targeted his family Actor Manoj Bajpayee said the backlash over Ghooskhor Pandat brought threats, abuse and attacks on his family. He said the team apologised quickly, agreed to change the title, but felt people reacted without knowing the film's subject.

Brown trailer: Karisma Kapoor fights inner turmoil while solving a murder The trailer of Brown introduces Karisma Kapoor as a troubled police officer investigating a teenager's murder in Kolkata. The Zee5 series also stars Jisshu Sengupta and Soni Razdan, among others.

From Tejasswi's 'shut up' to Karan's proposal: Inside the making of Desi Bling Desi Bling producer Mazen Laham reveals how the Netflix reality show was cast and whether the drama was scripted. He also shared behind-the-scenes details of Tejasswi's viral 'shut uppp' moment and Karan Kundrra's proposal.

Why Arun Govil believes Ranbir Kapoor's Ramayana faces an uphill battle Actor Arun Govil said his long association with Lord Rama shaped reactions to Ranbir Kapoor's first Ramayana glimpse. He also explained why he hesitated before joining the film as Lord Dasharath.

Ram Charan congratulates Tamil Nadu CM Vijay: You left stardom, career at peak Actor Ram Charan congratulated actor-turned-politician Vijay on becoming the Chief Minister of Tamil Nadu, calling it a proud moment for the film industry and the people of Tamil Nadu. He said Vijay had stepped away from stardom and his career at its peak.

Obsession on OTT: When, where to watch horror sleeper hit everyone's talking about Michael Johnston and Inde Navarrette's psychological horror thriller has emerged as a surprise box-office success. Here's when the film will head to OTT after its theatrical run.

Ram Charan on how Peddi changed him as an actor and person In an exclusive interview with India Today, Ram Charan said Peddi allowed him to represent people who need voice and recognition. He called it his toughest role and revealed he pushed through a worsening injury during the wrestling shoot.

Jackie Shroff joins Sanjay Dutt in Khalnayak Returns, confirms role Jackie Shroff has confirmed that he will return for Khalnayak Returns, reuniting with Sanjay Dutt in the sequel to the 1993 cult hit. The veteran actor said he has already spoken to Subhash Ghai, the producer and Dutt about the project.

Ban FWICE, not Ranveer Singh: Ram Gopal Varma backs actor, joins Don 3 controversy Ram Gopal Varma has attacked FWICE over its non-cooperation directive against Ranveer Singh in the Don 3 dispute. The clash has widened the controversy, with the federation defending its step and the actor's team choosing silence.

Actor Ajith Kumar's mother dies at 85. CM Vijay, Kamal Haasan, celebs pay tribute Tamil actor Ajith Kumar's mother, Mohini Mani, died at the age of 85 after battling age-related health issues. The film fraternity, political leaders and fans across India have expressed their condolences, honouring her memory.

Obsession takeover: A Rs 7 crore horror film just exposed Bollywood's biggest issue Curry Barker's low-budget horror film Obsession has become Hollywood's biggest ROI story of 2026. Its breakout run has renewed attention on Bollywood's shrinking space for mid-budget theatrical films.

Did Emilia Clarke earn $300,000 per episode on Game of Thrones? Actor reacts Emilia Clarke has challenged long-running claims that the main Game of Thrones cast earned $300,000 per episode. She played Daenerys Targaryen throughout the show's eight-season run.

Censor Board cuts 38 seconds from Obsession despite A rating, horror fans miffed Obsession opened in Indian theatres after the CBFC removed 38 seconds of footage despite an A certificate. The cuts triggered online criticism from viewers who said key plot elements were affected.

The curtain falls: David Dhawan's iconic No 1 Bollywood comedy era ends As David Dhawan calls it a day with Hai Jawani Toh Ishq Hona Hai, we revisit the maestro who turned madcap chaos into pure 90s entertainment — unapologetic, unforgettable, and now, a complex nostalgia.

Khushbu Sundar meets PM Narendra Modi, invites him to daughter Avantika's wedding Khushbu Sundar and Sundar C met Prime Minister Narendra Modi in New Delhi to invite him to daughter Avantika's wedding to Shravan Sreenivasan. The BJP member shared the photos on social media.

Ali Fazal's Raakh sets June premiere, new poster hints at dark mystery Actors Ali Fazal and Sonali Bendre's upcoming show Raakh will have a June premiere. Prime Video announced the release date with a gritty new poster.

Ranveer Singh meets Arsenal midfielder Declan Rice in Budapest amid Don 3 row Actor Ranveer Singh's latest photo with Arsenal midfielder Declan Rice in Budapest has sparked buzz online. The actor is currently is making headlines after Don 3 exit.

Varun Dhawan seeks protection of personality rights from Delhi High Court Actor Varun Dhawan has secured interim protection from the Delhi High Court against the misuse of his identity online. The order comes amid rising concerns over deepfakes and unauthorised digital content involving celebrities.

general news.headline: BJP wanted to kill me: Abhishek Banerjee's shirt torn, specs broken in mob attackTMC MP Abhishek Banerjee's visit to a Bengal post-poll victim's family in Sonarpur turned violent after a mob threw stones, eggs and assaulted him. The clash has sparked a fresh political confrontation, with the TMC blaming the BJP over "deteriorating law and order" and for doing "revenge politics".

GenZ whistleblower claims CBSE cooked tender to pick tainted OSM partnerControversy over CBSE's On-Screen Marking system deepened after a 17-year-old's investigation alleged that tender norms were diluted and security safeguards scaled back, raising questions about how Coempt Eduteck secured the contract

Vinesh Phogat's return to wrestling: What a messVinesh Phogat returned to the wrestling mat for the first time since the Paris Olympics, hoping to secure her place at the 2026 Asian Games. Instead, her comeback became a day-long confrontation with the Wrestling Federation of India.

Won't allow you to rest: Congress's message to Siddaramaiah as DKS takes chargeCongress made it clear that Siddaramaiah would continue to play a key political role in both state and national politics, declaring the party 'will not allow him to rest'.

Obsession takeover: A Rs 7 crore horror film just exposed Bollywood's biggest issueCurry Barker's low-budget horror film Obsession has become Hollywood's biggest ROI story of 2026. Its breakout run has renewed attention on Bollywood's shrinking space for mid-budget theatrical films.

Five-storey building crashes down in South Delhi, several feared buriedA routine Saturday evening turned into a race against time after a multi-storey building suddenly collapsed near Saket Metro station. With survivors already pulled from the debris, rescuers fear more people could still be trapped.

UCL Final Live: PSG beat Arsenal 4-3 on penalties to win Champions LeaguePSG vs Arsenal Live Score and Updates, UEFA Champions League Final: PSG have beaten Arsenal to defend their Champions League title. Gabriel's penalty miss hands PSG the title, and makes Luis Enrique a 3-time UCL winning manager.

India's brutal heatwave breaks Sunday, but storms replacing it are no picnicThe India Meteorological Department has forecast thunderstorms, hailstorms, and squally winds across large parts of India this Sunday, May 31, 2026. The southwest monsoon is also advancing and may reach Kerala within days.
Can you prove your Indian citizenship? I'm not sure I canCitizenship of India is a nice thought, a happy notion and a grand legal nuance. You think you have it, but do you, really?

TMC leader promises to return cut money, villagers blare reminders on loudspeakersVillagers in West Bengal's Cooch Behar district are using loudspeakers to remind a TMC leader of a promised "cut money" refund by June 4. They have threatened fresh protests if the money was not returned as promised by the deadline.

Man repeatedly slams toddler to ground, kills him to marry child's motherThe accused killed the one-and-a-half-year-old boy by repeatedly smashing him onto the ground, as he considered the toddler an obstacle to his relationship with the child's mother.

TMC-allied Tollywood actor gets arrest shield as Rograni post bites backWest Bengal's BJP government is reopening cases linked to the post-poll violence that followed the 2021 Assembly elections. Amid this, a five-year-old "Rograni Dibosh" post by actor Parambrata Chatterjee, made on the day the results were declared and violence erupted across parts of the state, has come back to haunt him. Though the Calcutta High Court has granted him anticipatory bail, the post has become a thorn in Chatterjee's side.

Ram Charan on how Peddi changed him as an actor and personIn an exclusive interview with India Today, Ram Charan said Peddi allowed him to represent people who need voice and recognition. He called it his toughest role and revealed he pushed through a worsening injury during the wrestling shoot.

Eggs, stones thrown at Abhishek Banerjee, TMC MP hospitalised after attackTMC MP Abhishek Banerjee was allegedly attacked during a visit to Sonarpur in West Bengal on Saturday, with eggs and stones thrown at his convoy as BJP workers raised "chor chor" slogans. Banerjee was visiting families affected by post-poll violence.

US keeps Iran strike option open as peace breakthrough remains elusiveUS Defence Secretary Pete Hegseth reiterated his country is prepared to launch fresh strikes on Iran if talks fail, as efforts to secure a peace agreement remain deadlocked despite months of diplomatic pressure on Tehran.

Abhishek has blood clots after attack, BJP forced hospital to discharge him: MamataMamata Banerjee alleged that BJP leaders and a senior police officer pressured hospitals to discharge Abhishek Banerjee despite his serious condition after the Sonarpur attack, claiming he developed blood clots and required intensive medical care.

In 1997, India's ocean saved the monsoon from El Nino. This year, it won'tIn 1997, one of the strongest El Ninos ever recorded could not break India's monsoon because the Indian Ocean pushed back. The IOD is flat this year. El Nino is still building. And the September peak is heading straight for India's standing crops.
Question Time: Weekly News QuizThink you read the headlines this week? Let's test that!

Man holds wife captive, beats her, forces 'murga' punishment for 4 hours; arrestedA Mumbai man with 19 criminal cases was arrested for assaulting his wife for hours, forcing her into a 'murga' position and confining her at home.

Why Siddaramaiah's dethroning might hit Rahul Gandhi the worstThe Congress high command might have resolved the Karnataka deadlock by asking Siddaramaiah to step down, but the move could backfire for it in the 2028 Assembly polls. The unseating of the veteran OBC leader could also blunt Rahul Gandhi's choicest of political weapons.

Rick roll, Orry, and now OSM: CBSE's technical nightmares in April-MayFrom a Rick Astley 'surprise link' in a Maths paper to an Orry QR code in the history paper and now glitches in the OSM system, CBSE's exam process has repeatedly run into digital trouble. While the board has issued clarifications on each, the incidents have left students confused and questioning the system's reliability.

India says BrahMos deal signed with Vietnam, Indonesia next in lineDefence Secretary Rajesh Kumar Singh said Vietnam has already signed a deal with India for the BrahMos supersonic cruise missile system, though it is yet to be publicly announced, while Indonesia is in the final stages of concluding a similar agreement.

DK Shivakumar unanimously elected CLP leader, set to take over as Karnataka CMWith the formalities over, the focus now shifts to who gets what in the incoming Cabinet. Maintaining caste, regional and factional balance will be key to DK Shivakumar's 23-month tenure as Chief Minister of Karnataka.

Para-athlete found dead in Ghaziabad days after qualifying for Para Asian GamesPara-athlete Chirag Tyagi's body was recovered from Ghaziabad, with a wound on his back. It is not yet clear whether the injury was caused by a bullet or another sharp object.

sports.headline: Defending champion Coco Gauff crashes out as Potapova delivers French Open shock Defending champion Coco Gauff's French Open title defence came to a stunning end on Saturday as Austria's Anastasia Potapova battled back from a set down to secure a 4-6, 7-6(1), 6-4 victory and advance to the fourth round at Roland Garros.

Vinesh Phogat's return to wrestling: What a mess Vinesh Phogat returned to the wrestling mat for the first time since the Paris Olympics, hoping to secure her place at the 2026 Asian Games. Instead, her comeback became a day-long confrontation with the Wrestling Federation of India.

Freya Kemp powers England past India to level women's T20I series Freya Kemp starred with bat and ball as England beat India by 26 runs in the second Women's T20I in Bristol on Saturday to level the three-match series 1-1. The series-deciding third T20I will be played in Taunton on Tuesday.

IPL 2026 final: Gujarat's Ahmedabad arrival delayed until Sat eve due to storm A day before the IPL 2026 final, Gujarat Titans were forced to alter their travel plans after stormy weather delayed their departure from Chandigarh, leaving them with little time to settle in Ahmedabad ahead of Sunday's clash against Royal Challengers Bengaluru.

RCB vs GT: Gujarat Titans' Shubman Gill looks to exploit home advantage in IPL final IPL 2026 Final, RCB vs GT: Standing on the verge of winning his first IPL title as captain, Gujarat Titans skipper Shubman Gill said he is not weighed down by expectations and believes his side can benefit from playing the final at home against Royal Challengers Bengaluru on Sunday.

UCL Final Live: PSG beat Arsenal 4-3 on penalties to win Champions League PSG vs Arsenal Live Score and Updates, UEFA Champions League Final: PSG have beaten Arsenal to defend their Champions League title. Gabriel's penalty miss hands PSG the title, and makes Luis Enrique a 3-time UCL winning manager.

Rajat Patidar reveals secret behind RCB's push for consecutive IPL titles Rajat Patidar has revealed the captaincy mantra behind Royal Challengers Bengaluru's success as they stand just one win away from retaining their IPL title. While victory over Gujarat Titans in Sunday's final would see him join MS Dhoni and Rohit Sharma as the only captains to successfully defend an IPL crown, Patidar said he has always preferred to lead in his own way rather than follow the path set by others.

Sabalenka powers past Kasatkina, books fourth-round date with Osaka Aryna Sabalenka continued her strong run at the French Open, beating Australia's Daria Kasatkina 6-0, 7-5 on Saturday to reach the fourth round at Roland Garros and set up a blockbuster clash with four-time Grand Slam champion Naomi Osaka.

Vinesh Phogat stands proud after Asian Games trials: Fighting the whole system Defiant despite missing an Asian Games berth, Vinesh Phogat declared she won her true victory by overcoming administrative hurdles and returning to the mat as a mother. The former world medalist slammed the wrestling system while confirming her target remains the 2028 Olympics.

Will the IPL final answer what Gill and Patidar won't about T20I selection? On the eve of the IPL 2026 final, RCB's Rajat Patidar stonewalled and GT's Shubman Gill chose his words carefully. Both have made their case with the bat when it comes to T20I selection chatter Now the biggest game of the year must do the rest.

Vaibhav Sooryavanshi is world's best batter right now: Shubman GillIPL 2026: Gujarat Titans captain Shubman Gill called Rajasthan Royals wonderkid Vaibhav Sooryavanshi the world's best batter right now after their Qualifier 2 clash. Despite hitting a winning century himself, Gill was left in absolute awe of the 15-year-old's fearless mindset.

Singapore Open: Satwik-Chirag stun world no. 1 Seo-Kim to enter finalSatwiksairaj Rankireddy and Chirag Shetty produced a superb display to beat World No. 1 pair Seo Seungjae and Kim Wonho in straight games and reach the men's doubles final of the Singapore Open on Saturday.

Liverpool sack head coach Arne Slot after dismal Premier League title defenceLiverpool have sacked Arne Slot after a disappointing 2025-26 season that saw the club finish fifth in the Premier League. The Dutchman departs just a year after leading the Reds to the league title in his debut campaign at Anfield.

Main wapas aaungi: Vinesh exits Asian Games trials after semi-final defeatVinesh Phogat was knocked out of the Asian Games selection trials here on Saturday after losing to Meenakshi Goyat 4-6 in a tense semifinal bout of the women's 53kg event.

Asian games trials: Mega feud breaks out between Vinesh Phogat's camp and WFIVinesh Phogat beat Nishu 7-6 in a 53kg qualification trial that lurched from contest to confrontation over scoring calls. The stoppages, broken review screen and sideline clashes exposed the administrative disorder still hanging over Indian wrestling.

No SKY, no Gill: Vaibhav Sooryavanshi headlines India's Asian Games probablesThe BCCI has submitted a 30-member men's probables list for the Asian Games in Aichi-Nagoya, with teenage sensation Vaibhav Sooryavanshi earning a spot while India captains Shubman Gill and Suryakumar Yadav have been left out.

Cristiano Ronaldo to play 2030 home World Cup? Portugal head coach believes soFIFA World Cup: Roberto Martinez has refused to rule out Cristiano Ronaldo playing at the 2030 World Cup. The remark underlines his extraordinary longevity and Portugal's continuing reliance on his experience, authority and leadership.

WWE star heaps praise on 15-year-old Vaibhav Sooryavanshi despite loss vs GTIPL 2026: Drew McIntyre hailed Vaibhav Sooryavanshi after another dazzling Qualifier 2 innings despite Rajasthan Royals' exit. The praise underlined how a 15-year-old's Orange Cap season has pushed him into cricket's global conversation.

Day 7 Live: Sabalenka through to fourth round, Anisimova, Gauff crash outFrench Open Day 7 Live Updates: Defending champion Coco Gauff crashes out after a stunning third-round defeat to Anastasia Potapova. Meanwhile, World No. 1 Aryna Sabalenka moved into the fourth round of Roland Garros with a 6-0, 7-5 win over Daria Kasatkina on Saturday. The Belarusian dominated the opening set before overcoming a tougher challenge in the second to book her place in the last 16. Sabalenka will now face Naomi Osaka, who reached the fourth round of Roland Garros for the first time in her career after defeating Iva Jovic.

Don't forget Vinesh Phogat: Superfan inks wrestler's face before Asian Games trialsVinesh Phogat entered the Asian Games trials after the Supreme Court let a Delhi High Court order stand and fans turned up to back her. Her fight with the federation has become bigger than selection, exposing how returning athletes can still be forced to battle the system first.

Day 7 schedule: Aryna Sabalenka, Coco Gauff eye prequarters berthDay 7 at the French Open 2026 features a star-studded third-round schedule, with Aryna Sabalenka and Coco Gauff leading the charge as they aim for pre-quarterfinal spots at Roland Garros.

Barcelona shut down critics, seal 80 million euro Anthony Gordan transferBarcelona unveiled Anthony Gordon after sealing his big-money move from Newcastle United. The transfer underlines their push for more attacking depth and delivers a childhood ambition the England winger says he never abandoned.

Champions League: Arsenal face PSG with European glory and history on the lineArsenal meet holders PSG in the Champions League final in Budapest after dominant but contrasting runs. A first European crown for Arsenal or a rare successful defence for PSG would make this a defining final.

Djokovic was asked about his future after French Open exit. The Serbian was bluntFrench Open 2026: 14-time Grand Slam champion Novak Djokovic spoke about his future after a shock third-round exit at Roland Garros, where Brazil's Joao Fonseca defeated him in a five-set, five-hour marathon clash on Centre Court.

How Gurindervir took back the title of fastest Indian alive from Animesh KujurGurindervir Singh and Animesh Kujur are two of the fastest men in India. The battle between the two is rapidly taking sprinting to new heights in the country.

Delayed entry, weight change: Vinesh Phogat clears last-minute hurdles for Asian Games trialsVinesh Phogat was finally cleared to compete in the Asian Games 53kg trials on Saturday after days of legal and administrative confusion involving the Wrestling Federation of India.

Vaibhav Sooryavanshi more than ready: Sangakkara backs RR starboy for India call-upIPL 2026, GT vs RR: Kumar Sangakkara believes Rajasthan Royals teen sensation Vaibhav Sooryavanshi is ready for an India call-up after a record-breaking IPL 2026 season in which he scored 776 runs and is the Orange Cap holder.

Arsene Wenger built the dream, can Arteta complete it with Champions League glory?As Arsenal prepare for a defining Champions League final against PSG, the debate around Mikel Arteta's place in the club's history has intensified. Former India international Robin Singh breaks down Arsenal's rebuild, Wenger's enduring legacy, and why this final could become the biggest test of Arteta's project so far.

Play of the Day: Gill, Sudharsan and a reminder that cricket's basics never go out of fashionIPL Qualifier 2, GT vs RR: In an IPL era obsessed with innovation, Shubman Gill and Sai Sudharsan turned back to cricket's oldest lessons. Their record-breaking partnership against Rajasthan Royals was a reminder that timing, technique and trust in the basics remain the sport's most reliable currency.

education.headline: The role of data analytics in modern advertising educationThis transformation also exercises a significant impact on the realm of 'advertising education.'

NCERT deletes Azad Pak, adds China's aggression reference in Class 12 textbookThe NCERT has made final changes to its Class 12 Political Science textbook syllabus for the upcoming academic year, including a revision in reference to India's border situation with China, Azad Pakistan, Article 370 and more.

NEET row: Bihar cops find 6 post-dated cheques 'issued for question paper brokers'Six post-dated cheques which were suspected to have been issued in favour of mafia demanding over Rs 30 lakh from each candidate seeking alleged leaked question paper ahead of NEET held last month were recovered by Bihar Police.

Forest fires surge tenfold from 2004 to 2017: Causes, impact and preventionAmidst a relentless surge, intense forest fires are now multiplying tenfold, casting a menacing shadow over our flora and fauna. Year by year, their fiery grip tightens, threatening ecosystems far and wide, now extending their reach to impact human lives.

Fresh plea in Supreme Court to recall NEET results, reconduct examsA fresh petition has been filed in the Supreme Court to recall the National Entrance cum Eligibility Test - Under Graduate (NEET-UG) 2024 results and re-conduct fresh examination after discrepancies in the declared results.

NTA issues clarification after NEET result controversy over toppers, high cut-offIn a detailed statement, the National Testing Agency (NTA) said that the increase in cut-off and number of toppers in the NEET-UG reflect the competitive nature of the exam and maintained that the test's integrity has not been compromised.

Exclusive: Arvind Kejriwal claims 'BJP succession war, PM backing Amit Shah'Speaking exclusively to India Today TV, Delhi Chief Minister Arvind Kejriwal claimed that there is a "succession war" within the BJP. He also claimed that the ruling party is "not fighting the Lok Sabha elections as a team".

4 medical students arrested in Bihar for appearing as proxies for NEET aspirantsFour MBBS students who appeared in place of NEET aspirants in the medical entrance exam were detained in Bihar's Purnea.

NEET-UG question paper not leaked, claims baseless: National Testing AgencyThe reports claiming question paper leak in the medical entrance exam NEET-UG are "completely baseless and without any ground", the National Testing Agency (NTA) has clarified.
The generation with answers: Tech-savvy Indian youth tackling global challengesIndia's tech-savvy youth rewriting the global narrative, one solution at a time.

Left sweeps JNU students' polls, Dhananjay is first Dalit presidentThe Left swept the Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls, winning all four posts and trouncing the RSS-affiliated ABVP. The ABVP was leading at one point, but the Left eventually surged ahead and emerged victorious.
The power of knowledge: Education's influence on real estate market trendsIn a dynamic sector like real estate, investing in continuing education is not simply an option; it is a requirement.

JNU students' body election on March 22 after 4 yearsThe Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls will take place on March 22, more than four years after the last elections were held in September 2019.

UP 12th board exam math, biology papers leaked on WhatsApp, complaint filedThe mathematics and biology question papers for the Uttar Pradesh class 12 board exam were allegedly leaked on a WhatsApp group.

UP Police Constable recruitment exam cancelled days after paper leakThe Uttar Pradesh Police Constable recruitment exam held on February 17 and 18 was canceled due to a paper leak. The Chief Minister has announced a re-examination within the next six months.
